repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Related: #32280, #31621

## Problem

`:edit <dir>` and `nvim <dir>` currently rely on netrw to show local directory contents.

## Solution

Add a small builtin Lua directory listing for local directory buffers, with `filetype=directory`, entry opening, parent navigation, and refresh.

`netrw` remains available for `:Explore`, remote paths, archives, and file operations.

comment_data.body:
should we go ahead and introduce a `filetype=directory` ? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/31621

> |:edit| on a directory path now opens |nvim-directory|

this is a strange phrasing, would suggest maybe `The builtin |nvim-directory| plugin is used to present directory contents in a buffer.`

Also generally, we should deemphasize the plugin aspect. Nvim simply "shows directories". 

> |netrw| remains available for |:Explore| and remote file operations.

this is temporary i assume/hope
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -253,6 +253,8 @@ CHANGED FEATURES                                                 *news-changed*
 
 These existing features changed their behavior.
 
+• |:edit| on a directory path now opens |nvim-directory|. |netrw| remains
+  available for |:Explore| and remote file operations.

comment_data.body: :+1: to `filetype=directory` as long as it would be future-compatible with remote directories (via plugin) too
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -253,6 +253,8 @@ CHANGED FEATURES                                                 *news-changed*
 
 These existing features changed their behavior.
 
+• |:edit| on a directory path now opens |nvim-directory|. |netrw| remains
+  available for |:Explore| and remote file operations.

comment_data.body: Could you try to move the setup() function here in order to lazy-load the module? I.e. having the autocmds defined in the plugin file
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+if vim.g.loaded_nvim_directory_plugin ~= nil then
+  return
+end
+vim.g.loaded_nvim_directory_plugin = true
+
+require('nvim.directory').setup()

comment_data.body:
Special characters shouldn't be escaped with `\x` notation, but instead Vim's typical [`^x` caret notation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caret_notation) (or as `<XX>` when using `:set display+=uhex`). You shouldn't need any code to do this, just insert the raw characters directly into the buffer and let Vim's renderer handle it.

The only thing that will need to be actually changed is `\n`, which gets translated to `\0`, as you are doing.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,331 @@
+--- @brief
+--- Directory listing for `:edit <dir>`.
+---
+--- The plugin can be disabled by setting `g:loaded_nvim_directory_plugin = 1`.
+
+local api = vim.api
+local fs = vim.fs
+local uv = vim.uv
+
+local M = {}
+
+---@class nvim.directory.Entry
+---@field display string
+---@field name string
+---@field path string
+---@field type string
+---@field parent? boolean
+
+---@class nvim.directory.State
+---@field dir string
+---@field entries nvim.directory.Entry[]
+
+---@type table<integer,nvim.directory.State>
+local states = {}
+
+---@type fun(path: string, opts?: { buf?: integer })
+local open
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_entry
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_parent
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local refresh
+
+---@param path string
+---@return string
+local function normalize_dir(path)
+  return fs.normalize(fs.abspath(path))
+end
+
+---@param path string
+---@return boolean
+local function is_dir(path)
+  local stat = uv.fs_stat(path)
+  return stat ~= nil and stat.type == 'directory'
+end
+
+---@param name string
+---@return string
+local function display_name(name)
+  return (
+    name:gsub('\\', '\\\\'):gsub('[%z\1-\31\127]', function(char)
+      if char == '\t' then
+        return '\\t'
+      elseif char == '\r' then
+        return '\\r'

comment_data.body: should probably be `R`. CTRL-L is an escape hatch for clearing display artifacts, search highlights, etc., that the user expects to work in any buffer.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,331 @@
+--- @brief
+--- Directory listing for `:edit <dir>`.
+---
+--- The plugin can be disabled by setting `g:loaded_nvim_directory_plugin = 1`.
+
+local api = vim.api
+local fs = vim.fs
+local uv = vim.uv
+
+local M = {}
+
+---@class nvim.directory.Entry
+---@field display string
+---@field name string
+---@field path string
+---@field type string
+---@field parent? boolean
+
+---@class nvim.directory.State
+---@field dir string
+---@field entries nvim.directory.Entry[]
+
+---@type table<integer,nvim.directory.State>
+local states = {}
+
+---@type fun(path: string, opts?: { buf?: integer })
+local open
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_entry
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_parent
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local refresh
+
+---@param path string
+---@return string
+local function normalize_dir(path)
+  return fs.normalize(fs.abspath(path))
+end
+
+---@param path string
+---@return boolean
+local function is_dir(path)
+  local stat = uv.fs_stat(path)
+  return stat ~= nil and stat.type == 'directory'
+end
+
+---@param name string
+---@return string
+local function display_name(name)
+  return (
+    name:gsub('\\', '\\\\'):gsub('[%z\1-\31\127]', function(char)
+      if char == '\t' then
+        return '\\t'
+      elseif char == '\r' then
+        return '\\r'
+      elseif char == '\n' then
+        return '\0'
+      end
+      return ('\\x%02x'):format(char:byte())
+    end)
+  )
+end
+
+---@param dir string
+---@return nvim.directory.Entry[]
+---@return string? err
+local function read_entries(dir)
+  ---@type nvim.directory.Entry[]
+  local entries = {}
+
+  local parent = fs.dirname(dir)
+  if parent ~= dir then
+    entries[#entries + 1] = {
+      display = '../',
+      name = '..',
+      path = parent,
+      type = 'directory',
+      parent = true,
+    }
+  end
+
+  local scan, err = uv.fs_scandir(dir)
+  if not scan then
+    return entries, err or 'cannot read directory'
+  end
+
+  ---@type nvim.directory.Entry[]
+  local children = {}
+
+  while true do
+    local name, type, _ = uv.fs_scandir_next(scan)
+    if not name then
+      if type then
+        return entries, type
+      end
+      break
+    end
+    local path = fs.joinpath(dir, name)
+    if type == 'link' and is_dir(path) then
+      type = 'directory'
+    end
+    children[#children + 1] = {
+      display = display_name(name) .. (type == 'directory' and '/' or ''),
+      name = name,
+      path = path,
+      type = type,
+    }
+  end
+
+  table.sort(children, function(a, b)
+    if (a.type == 'directory') ~= (b.type == 'directory') then
+      return a.type == 'directory'
+    end
+    return a.name < b.name
+  end)
+  vim.list_extend(entries, children)
+
+  return entries
+end
+
+---@param buf integer
+---@param dir string
+---@param entries nvim.directory.Entry[]
+local function set_lines(buf, dir, entries)
+  local lines = {} ---@type string[]
+  for i, entry in ipairs(entries) do
+    lines[i] = entry.display
+  end
+
+  vim.bo[buf].modeline = false
+  vim.bo[buf].buftype = 'nowrite'
+  vim.bo[buf].bufhidden = 'delete'
+  vim.bo[buf].buflisted = false
+  vim.bo[buf].swapfile = false
+  vim.bo[buf].readonly = false
+  vim.bo[buf].modifiable = true
+  api.nvim_buf_set_name(buf, dir)
+  api.nvim_buf_set_lines(buf, 0, -1, false, lines)
+  vim.bo[buf].modified = false
+  vim.bo[buf].readonly = true
+  vim.bo[buf].modifiable = false
+end
+
+---@param buf integer
+local function set_maps(buf)
+  vim.keymap.set('n', '<CR>', function()
+    open_entry(buf)
+  end, { buf = buf, silent = true, desc = 'Open directory entry' })
+
+  vim.keymap.set('n', '-', function()
+    open_parent(buf)
+  end, { buf = buf, silent = true, desc = 'Open parent directory' })
+
+  vim.keymap.set('n', '<C-L>', function()
+    refresh(buf)
+  end, { buf = buf, silent = true, desc = 'Refresh directory' })

comment_data.body: why wasn't `vim.fs.dir()` sufficient/usful here?

comment_data.body: ok a 1-line code-comment mentioning that is very much worth the cost. saves time and avoids dead ends later.

comment_data.body: I want to take a step back and consider: what should the buffer text here actually represent? It should not be a cobbled-together language of special string parsings halfway between user-facing filenames and normal buffer behavior. Intuitively, I think it should look exactly like _regular_ buffers, as well as respect the display options accordingly. Regular buffers have the following behavior: ``` raw ^A -> ^A, or <01> with display+=uhex raw CR -> ^M, or <0d> raw DEL -> ^?, or <7f> raw TAB -> spacing, or list/listchars visualization raw NL -> a newline (exception) ``` The only "problem" with this solution is that tabs will render (because of `nolist`), as spacing. This informs my opinions on `strtrans()` or other functions meant for keys: using these would freeze the buffer, disallowing subsequent visual updates to the buffer from changes in options (`display`, `listchars`). We'd have to re-render on those option changes. Here's what my solution would look like. I haven't pushed it yet:

+---@class nvim.directory.Entry

+---@type fun(buf: integer)

+      elseif char == '\r' then

It would've been nice if vim.fs.dir returned an ok, result type-value for this purpose :/

@@ -0,0 +1,331 @@
+--- @brief
+--- Directory listing for `:edit <dir>`.
+---
+--- The plugin can be disabled by setting `g:loaded_nvim_directory_plugin = 1`.
+
+local api = vim.api
+local fs = vim.fs
+local uv = vim.uv
+
+local M = {}
+
+---@class nvim.directory.Entry
+---@field display string
+---@field name string
+---@field path string
+---@field type string
+---@field parent? boolean
+
+---@class nvim.directory.State
+---@field dir string
+---@field entries nvim.directory.Entry[]
+
+---@type table<integer,nvim.directory.State>
+local states = {}
+
+---@type fun(path: string, opts?: { buf?: integer })
+local open
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_entry
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_parent
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local refresh
+
+---@param path string
+---@return string
+local function normalize_dir(path)
+  return fs.normalize(fs.abspath(path))
+end
+
+---@param path string
+---@return boolean
+local function is_dir(path)
+  local stat = uv.fs_stat(path)
+  return stat ~= nil and stat.type == 'directory'
+end
+
+---@param name string
+---@return string
+local function display_name(name)
+  return (
+    name:gsub('\\', '\\\\'):gsub('[%z\1-\31\127]', function(char)
+      if char == '\t' then
+        return '\\t'
+      elseif char == '\r' then
+        return '\\r'
+      elseif char == '\n' then
+        return '\0'
+      end
+      return ('\\x%02x'):format(char:byte())
+    end)
+  )
+end
+
+---@param dir string
+---@return nvim.directory.Entry[]
+---@return string? err
+local function read_entries(dir)
+  ---@type nvim.directory.Entry[]
+  local entries = {}
+
+  local parent = fs.dirname(dir)
+  if parent ~= dir then
+    entries[#entries + 1] = {
+      display = '../',
+      name = '..',
+      path = parent,
+      type = 'directory',
+      parent = true,
+    }
+  end
+
+  local scan, err = uv.fs_scandir(dir)

```suggestion
      { 'bufhidden', 'unload' },
```

`'bufhidden'` being "delete" will cause the buffer to be unlisted when it is hidden.

@@ -0,0 +1,339 @@
+--- @brief
+--- Directory listing for `:edit <dir>`.
+---
+--- The plugin can be disabled by setting `g:loaded_nvim_directory_plugin = 1`.
+
+local api = vim.api
+local fs = vim.fs
+local uv = vim.uv
+
+local M = {}
+
+---@class nvim.directory.Entry
+---@field display string
+---@field name string
+---@field path string
+---@field type string
+---@field parent? boolean
+
+---@class nvim.directory.State
+---@field dir string
+---@field entries nvim.directory.Entry[]
+
+---@type table<integer,nvim.directory.State>
+local states = {}
+
+---@type fun(path: string, opts?: { buf?: integer })
+local open
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_entry
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_parent
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local refresh
+
+---@param buf integer
+---@param options [string, any][]
+---@return boolean
+local function set_buf_options(buf, options)
+  for _, option in ipairs(options) do
+    if not api.nvim_buf_is_valid(buf) then
+      return false
+    end
+    api.nvim_set_option_value(option[1], option[2], { buf = buf })
+  end
+  return api.nvim_buf_is_valid(buf)
+end
+
+---@param path string
+---@return string
+local function normalize_dir(path)
+  return fs.normalize(fs.abspath(path))
+end
+
+---@param path string
+---@return boolean
+local function is_dir(path)
+  local stat = uv.fs_stat(path)
+  return stat ~= nil and stat.type == 'directory'
+end
+
+---@param name string
+---@return string
+local function display_name(name)
+  return (
+    name:gsub('\\', '\\\\'):gsub('[%z\1-\31\127]', function(char)
+      if char == '\t' then
+        return '\\t'
+      elseif char == '\r' then
+        return '\\r'
+      elseif char == '\n' then
+        return '\0'
+      end
+      return ('\\x%02x'):format(char:byte())
+    end)
+  )
+end
+
+---@param dir string
+---@return nvim.directory.Entry[]
+---@return string? err
+local function read_entries(dir)
+  ---@type nvim.directory.Entry[]
+  local entries = {}
+
+  local parent = fs.dirname(dir)
+  if parent ~= dir then
+    entries[#entries + 1] = {
+      display = '../',
+      name = '..',
+      path = parent,
+      type = 'directory',
+      parent = true,
+    }
+  end
+
+  -- Use uv directly (instead of vim.fs.dir()) so scan errors can be reported.
+  local scan, err = uv.fs_scandir(dir)
+  if not scan then
+    return entries, err or 'cannot read directory'
+  end
+
+  ---@type nvim.directory.Entry[]
+  local children = {}
+
+  while true do
+    local name, type, _ = uv.fs_scandir_next(scan)
+    if not name then
+      if type then
+        return entries, type
+      end
+      break
+    end
+    local path = fs.joinpath(dir, name)
+    if type == 'link' and is_dir(path) then
+      type = 'directory'
+    end
+    children[#children + 1] = {
+      display = display_name(name) .. (type == 'directory' and '/' or ''),
+      name = name,
+      path = path,
+      type = type,
+    }
+  end
+
+  table.sort(children, function(a, b)
+    if (a.type == 'directory') ~= (b.type == 'directory') then
+      return a.type == 'directory'
+    end
+    return a.name < b.name
+  end)
+  vim.list_extend(entries, children)
+
+  return entries
+end
+
+---@param buf integer
+---@param dir string
+---@param entries nvim.directory.Entry[]
+---@return boolean
+local function set_lines(buf, dir, entries)
+  local lines = {} ---@type string[]
+  for i, entry in ipairs(entries) do
+    lines[i] = entry.display
+  end
+
+  if
+    not set_buf_options(buf, {
+      { 'modeline', false },
+      { 'buftype', 'nowrite' },
+      { 'bufhidden', 'delete' },

+local function display_name(name)
+  return (
+    name:gsub('\\', '\\\\'):gsub('[%z\1-\31\127]', function(char)
+      if char == '\t' then
+        return '\\t'
+      elseif char == '\r' then
+        return '\\r'
+      elseif char == '\n' then
+        return '\0'
+      end
+      return ('\\x%02x'):format(char:byte())
+    end)
+  )
+end
+
+---@param dir string
+---@return nvim.directory.Entry[]
+---@return string? err
+local function read_entries(dir)
+  ---@type nvim.directory.Entry[]
+  local entries = {}
+
+  local parent = fs.dirname(dir)
+  if parent ~= dir then
+    entries[#entries + 1] = {
+      display = '../',
+      name = '..',
+      path = parent,
+      type = 'directory',
+      parent = true,
+    }
+  end
+
+  -- Use uv directly (instead of vim.fs.dir()) so scan errors can be reported.
+  local scan, err = uv.fs_scandir(dir)
+  if not scan then
+    return entries, err or 'cannot read directory'
+  end
+
+  ---@type nvim.directory.Entry[]
+  local children = {}
+
+  while true do
+    local name, type, _ = uv.fs_scandir_next(scan)
+    if not name then
+      if type then
+        return entries, type
+      end
+      break
+    end
+    local path = fs.joinpath(dir, name)
+    if type == 'link' and is_dir(path) then
+      type = 'directory'
+    end
+    children[#children + 1] = {
+      display = display_name(name) .. (type == 'directory' and '/' or ''),
+      name = name,
+      path = path,
+      type = type,
+    }
+  end
+
+  table.sort(children, function(a, b)
+    if (a.type == 'directory') ~= (b.type == 'directory') then
+      return a.type == 'directory'
+    end
+    return a.name < b.name
+  end)
+  vim.list_extend(entries, children)
+
+  return entries
+end
+
+---@param buf integer
+---@param dir string
+---@param entries nvim.directory.Entry[]
+---@return boolean
+local function set_lines(buf, dir, entries)
+  local lines = {} ---@type string[]
+  for i, entry in ipairs(entries) do
+    lines[i] = entry.display
+  end
+
+  if
+    not set_buf_options(buf, {
+      { 'modeline', false },
+      { 'buftype', 'nowrite' },
+      { 'bufhidden', 'delete' },

comment_data.body: I've implemented this solution.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,331 @@
+--- @brief
+--- Directory listing for `:edit <dir>`.
+---
+--- The plugin can be disabled by setting `g:loaded_nvim_directory_plugin = 1`.
+
+local api = vim.api
+local fs = vim.fs
+local uv = vim.uv
+
+local M = {}
+
+---@class nvim.directory.Entry
+---@field display string
+---@field name string
+---@field path string
+---@field type string
+---@field parent? boolean
+
+---@class nvim.directory.State
+---@field dir string
+---@field entries nvim.directory.Entry[]

+
+---@type fun(path: string, opts?: { buf?: integer })
+local open
+---@type fun(buf: integer)

+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local open_parent
+---@type fun(buf: integer)
+local refresh
+
+---@param path string
+---@return string
+local function normalize_dir(path)
+  return fs.normalize(fs.abspath(path))
+end
+
+---@param path string
+---@return boolean
+local function is_dir(path)
+  local stat = uv.fs_stat(path)
+  return stat ~= nil and stat.type == 'directory'
+end
+

+    name:gsub('\\', '\\\\'):gsub('[%z\1-\31\127]', function(char)
+      if char == '\t' then
+        return '\\t'
+      elseif char == '\r' then
+        return '\\r'

comment_regular_data.body: Of note for this implementation: filenames which contain line feeds characters (`\n`) should render as `^@`/`<00>`, despite not being null bytes (see #39408, closed as not planned). It should be as simple as `file_name:gsub('\n', '\0')`.
Ah, this case is unambiguous. Thanks for the notice - just updated.

does it make sense to introduce `filetype=directory` in a separate PR so we can close https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/31621 ?

also generally goes faster if we do things incrementally instead of 1 big pr
With recent fixes to filepath handling, this is now ready for review.

For some reason the OptionSet autocommand for buftype does not trigger for this plugin (run `nvim /` and nothing will be printed):

```lua
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('OptionSet', {
  pattern = 'buftype',
  callback = function()
    vim.print('optionset: ' .. vim.bo.buftype)
  end,
})
```

I'm not sure why the very explicit `vim.bo[buf].buftype = 'nowrite'` doesn't work, but it doesn't.

P.S. imo we shouldn't set `'nobuflisted'` for this plugin, unlike netrw. But that is a decision we can make later in a separate PR; I don't want to delay this PR.

thanks - added `nested = true` on the directory buffer autocmds and a test.

EDIT: I made it `buflisted` - I agree and sticking to netrw here (the original motivation) doesn't really make sense when it *should* be `buflisted`.
@superatomic the UI changes in the thread with the other maintainers is not addressed yet and thus not added to the pr; might be useful to revoke your review so it doesn't get merged incidentally

Oops, setting `'bufhidden'` to `unload` will break the syntax highlighting when you return to the buffer. You'll have to either set it to "hide" or fix the syntax highlighting. Sorry for not catching this originally.
